dossier.bio: Mr. Benjamin Shyong — founder and CEO of OpenClaw / Injester, a returning guest at Rosewood Hong Kong on his third stay this year. Quiet, ritual-driven; an Affluential Explorer who tunes his rhythm to the city he's in.
conversationHooks: Returning for FinTech Week again — he keynoted Oct 2025 (public). Treat as natural context, no need to introduce.
He's previously expressed interest in a private Lock Cha session with Selena Lai — he asked about it at checkout last visit.
handleWithCare: Dietary: vegetarian (confirmed via direct intake; older 2024 CRM tag of pescatarian is stale).
Tree-nut allergy is a hard constraint — confirm with kitchen on every plated item, including amenity and turndown.
Prefers silence and quiet; no in-room music, no welcome playlist. Sandalwood scent is OK at low intensity; he doesn't mind it but didn't ask for it.

suppressed.reason: Calendar overlay on past stays inferred deal-related meetings; below standard floor for staff context — keep as 'business focus' not specifics.
Granular per-amenity reactions across properties feel surveillance-y; aggregate to 'returning, knows the brand' instead.
Public-record co-investors and co-keynote speakers should not surface unprompted; private until guest raises.

reasoning: Voice / A2A intake confirmed roomTempC = 20 this season (CRM had 22°C default; the 20°C is fresher).
Prior HK stay (Mar 2025): requested down-free pillows on arrival — extend to bedding standard.
Stated preference: silence — suppress in-room music and welcome playlist.
Scent default reset to neutral per intake — sandalwood was a property default he didn't request.
Lighting kept at property default (ambient), dimmed for late afternoon arrival.

welcomeAmenity.name: Tea flight — three estates
welcomeAmenity.story: Curated by Lock Cha — Hong Kong's most respected tea master
welcomeAmenity.reasoning: Vegetarian, tree-nut-safe, and alcohol-free. Builds on his prior request (asked about a private Lock Cha session at last checkout) — pre-positioning this amenity quietly opens the door for the Wing Lee Street ceremony later in the stay. Relationship Hospitality, not a transaction.

itinerary.title: Sunrise harbour walk + Bici filter coffee on return
itinerary.whyHere: Victoria Dockside boardwalk to Tsim Sha Tsui promenade — quiet at first light
itinerary.vendorOrPlace: Victoria Dockside boardwalk · in-suite filter coffee on return
itinerary.reasoning: His standing morning ritual is a long walk + filter coffee. Pre-position the equipment in-suite (Chemex stocked at HK per concierge note); the walk uses the property's own front door.

itinerary.title: Wing Lee Street private tea ceremony
itinerary.whyHere: Preserved 1950s street; Lock Cha private session
itinerary.vendorOrPlace: Wing Lee Street, Sheung Wan · Selena Lai
itinerary.reasoning: He asked about this at checkout last visit. Selena Lai (Lock Cha master, our Placemaker) confirmed availability. Honour the conversation he opened with us.

itinerary.title: Vegetarian tasting at Chaat
itinerary.whyHere: Michelin-starred; full vegetarian Indian tasting on request
itinerary.reasoning: Vegetarian + tree-nut allergy both fit Chaat's regional Indian register — chef Manav Tuli (Placemaker) flagged. Discovery for a returning guest who's already done Henry's and Spring Moon.

itinerary.title: Quiet workspace — Roof at Eight
itinerary.whyHere: Members-only Sky Lounge with harbour view; quiet hours 14:00–17:00
itinerary.vendorOrPlace: The Manor Club · Roof at Eight
itinerary.reasoning: He's on FinTech Week — expect 2-3 hours of focused work mid-afternoon. Pre-reserved table, no service interruptions unless he flags.
